PlainProphecy
HomeCompareAbout
Mission

About Plain Prophecy

A Christ-centred, denomination-neutral resource for Christians who want to understand biblical prophecy rigorously — without sensationalism, fear-mongering, or denominational recruitment.

What Plain Prophecy Is

Most prophecy resources fall into one of two traps: they're either sensationalist end-times platforms that generate anxiety rather than understanding, or they're denomination-marketing tools where the conclusion is determined before the evidence is examined.

Plain Prophecy exists to do neither. We take the Historicist framework — the prophetic method of the entire Protestant Reformation — and examine it the way a historian would: by checking every claim against independent evidence, acknowledging where interpretive steps require inference, and keeping Christ at the centre of every timeline.

The Historicist reading of Daniel and Revelation was the consensus of Luther, Calvin, Tyndale, Knox, Newton, Wesley, and every major Reformer for nearly 300 years. Evangelical futurism is an 1830s innovation with origins in Jesuit Counter-Reformation polemic. The evidence is not close — but we let it speak for itself.

What Plain Prophecy Is Not

  • Not SDA recruitment — the content stands or falls on evidence, not denominational affiliation
  • Not a fear platform — prophecy understood correctly produces watchfulness, not anxiety
  • Not sensationalist — we don't assign current world leaders to prophetic roles
  • Not infallible — we acknowledge genuine scholarly debate wherever it exists
  • Not anti-Catholic as a person — the prophetic analysis applies to institutional systems, not individuals
Method

Hermeneutical Principles

01
Scripture Interprets Scripture

We never force a conclusion back into a text. The day-year principle is explicitly mandated (Num 14:34; Ezek 4:6) — not invented. Any prophetic anchor must be verifiable from the Bible itself before being checked against history.

02
History Verifies Prophecy

Genuine prophecy must have verifiable fulfilments independent of denominational tradition. We cite archaeology, secular chronology, and peer-reviewed history — not just church commentary. Every date is checked.

03
Christ at the Centre

Every prophetic period anchors to who Jesus is: the Messiah of 457 BC, the Sacrifice of 31 AD, the risen High Priest of 1844, the Coming King. Prophecy is not a news ticker — it is Christology unfolded in time.

04
Intellectual Honesty

We acknowledge where the Historicist position carries genuine scholarly debate. A 9/10 is not a failure — it reflects where additional inference is required. We score both traditions against the same evidentiary standard.

The Central Texts
Dan 8:14 · Dan 9:24–27 · Dan 7:25 · Rev 12:6, 14 · Rev 13:5 · Rev 14:6–12 · Rev 17–18 · Rev 20 · Heb 8–9
